step_by_step_reasoning: We identify the client devices (UserConsumerWeb) sending requests to API Gateway. This initiates a Lambda function which enqueues jobs in a top SQS queue. Spot-instance rendering engines running in an EC2 Auto Scaling Group consume jobs, upload video segments to S3, and push completion data to a bottom SQS queue. The frontend Lambda retrieves these playlist links for playback via CloudFront. Spot interruptions are handled using CloudWatch Events and a drainage Lambda, while custom scaling is managed by Step Functions triggering a metric-evaluation Lambda.

graph.name: SundaySky Personalized Video Rendering Architecture
graph.notes: A scalable video rendering architecture (Rendering as a Service - RAS) utilizing cost-efficient spot instances on EC2 managed via custom Step Functions scaling and automated Spot Interruption workflows.

nodes.name: Client Devices
nodes.notes: Web and mobile clients requesting personalized video content.

nodes.name: API Gateway
nodes.notes: Handles incoming requests, managing usage plans and API keys.

nodes.name: Frontend Lambda
nodes.notes: Validates requests and manages queue interaction.

nodes.name: Response SQS Queue
nodes.notes: Holds completed rendering results and playlists.

nodes.name: Rendering ASG
nodes.notes: Auto-scaling spot instances processing rendering jobs.

nodes.name: Video S3 Bucket
nodes.notes: Stores rendered video segments and playlists.

nodes.name: CloudFront CDN
nodes.notes: Caches and streams video segments to end users.

nodes.name: CloudWatch Events
nodes.notes: Monitors spot instances and collects custom queue metrics.

nodes.name: Interruption Lambda
nodes.notes: Drains active jobs from spot instances marked for termination.

nodes.name: Scaling Orchestrator
nodes.notes: Triggers scaling logic checks every 15 seconds.

nodes.name: Scaling Calculator
nodes.notes: Calculates required rendering capacity and updates ASG.

edges.notes: Pulls and returns playlist URL to client
Instructs targeted instance to drain and detach
